meaning: adj.不能防卫的,无辩护余地的
examples: The minister was accused of defending the indefensible.
The checkpoint was abandoned as militarily indefensible.
Symbolic of national unity as they claim to be, their very history—and sometimes the way they behave today—embodies outdated and indefensible privileges and inequalities.
To me it is obvious that what Entine seems out to defend is indefensible.
Because defending benefits that trap people in poverty and penalise work is defending the indefensible.
